paper.title: Attention is All you Need

openAccessPdf.disclaimer: Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use.

tldr.text: A new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely is proposed, which generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.

paper.abstract: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks in an encoder-decoder configuration. The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely. Experiments on two machine translation tasks show these models to be superior in quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train. Our model achieves 28.4 BLEU on the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task, improving over the existing best results, including ensembles by over 2 BLEU. On the WMT 2014 English-to-French translation task, our model establishes a new single-model state-of-the-art BLEU score of 41.8 after training for 3.5 days on eight GPUs, a small fraction of the training costs of the best models from the literature. We show that the Transformer generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.

arguments.query: transformer architecture ablation study component analysis

Hendrawan"}], "abstract": "Accurate cellular traffic prediction is crucial for capacity management, energy efficiency, and network slicing in 5G networks. This study proposes a hybrid model based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Transformer to predict network traffic using real operational Key Performance Indicators (KPI) data from 23 cells in Bandung over four months. This architecture combines the LSTM\u2019s ability to capture local temporal dependencies with the self-attention mechanism to identify global patterns, enriched by sinusoidal time embedding and representation fusion through a concatenation layer. Through systematic hyperparameter optimization, the model achieves R ${^{{2}}} =0.9357$ and Root Mean Squared (RMSE) = 0.9822, outperforming baselines such as Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA), LSTM-only, and Convolutional Long Short-Term Memory (ConvLSTM). Ablation studies prove the significant contribution of each architecture component, while robustness analysis demonstrates stability under incomplete data conditions. The novelty of the research lies in the application of a hybrid architecture to real KPI data, rather than simulated grid datasets. These results provide a strong basis for the application of traffic prediction in operational scenarios, such as base station sleeping and dynamic resource allocation. For further research, the integration of external factors and the development of lightweight models for edge deployment are highly recommended."}, {"paperId": "c4e7e8d4f564f4d0d8fbaedcc23d9e05dfa4d93f", "title": "MammoViT: A Custom Vision Transformer Architecture for Accurate BIRADS Classification in Mammogram Analysis", "year": 2025, "citationCount": 16, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "https://doi.org/10.3390/diagnostics15030285", "status": "GOLD", "license": "CCBY", "disclaimer": "Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11817779, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use."}, "authors": [{"authorId": "2342598773", "name": "Abdullah G. M. Al Mansour"}, {"authorId": "2309347297", "name": "Faisal Alshomrani"}, {"authorId": "2341770066", "name": "Abdullah Alfahaid"}, {"authorId": "2342630477", "name": "Abdulaziz T M Almutairi"}], "abstract": "Background: Breast cancer screening through mammography interpretation is crucial for early detection and improved patient outcomes. However, the manual classification of mammograms using the BIRADS (Breast Imaging-Reporting and Data System) remains challenging due to subtle imaging features, inter-reader variability, and increasing radiologist workload. Traditional computer-aided detection systems often struggle with complex feature extraction and contextual understanding of mammographic abnormalities. To address these limitations, this study proposes MammoViT, a novel hybrid deep learning framework that leverages both ResNet50\u2019s hierarchical feature extraction capabilities and Vision Transformer\u2019s ability to capture long-range dependencies in images. Methods: We implemented a multi-stage approach utilizing a pre-trained ResNet50 model for initial feature extraction from mammogram images. To address the significant class imbalance in our four-class BIRADS dataset, we applied SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) to generate synthetic samples for minority classes. The extracted feature arrays were transformed into non-overlapping patches with positional encodings for Vision Transformer processing. The Vision Transformer employs multi-head self-attention mechanisms to capture both local and global relationships between image patches, with each attention head learning different aspects of spatial dependencies. The model was optimized using Keras Tuner and trained using 5-fold cross-validation with early stopping to prevent overfitting. Results: MammoViT achieved 97.4% accuracy in classifying mammogram images across different BIRADS categories. The model\u2019s effectiveness was validated through comprehensive evaluation metrics, including a classification report, confusion matrix, probability distribution, and comparison with existing studies. Conclusions: MammoViT effectively combines ResNet50 and Vision Transformer architectures while addressing the challenge of imbalanced medical imaging datasets. The high accuracy and robust performance demonstrate its potential as a reliable tool for supporting clinical decision-making in breast cancer screening."}, {"paperId": "e7e3f94cd7278c3bf95433d4aee3be9850999371", "title": "Adaptive Renewable Energy Forecasting Utilizing a Data-Driven PCA-Transformer Architecture", "year": 2024, "citationCount": 17, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "https://doi.org/10.1109/access.2024.3440226", "status": "GOLD", "license": "CCBYNCND", "disclaimer": "Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://api.unpaywall.org/v2/10.1109/ACCESS.2024.3440226?email=<INSERT_YOUR_EMAIL> or https://doi.org/10.1109/ACCESS.2024.3440226, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use."}, "authors": [{"authorId": "2994258", "name": "Fahman Saeed"}, {"authorId": "2268015702", "name": "Sultan Aldera"}], "abstract": "The incorporation of renewable energy sources into the power grid has necessitated the development of sophisticated forecasting models that can effectively handle the inherent fluctuation and uncertainty associated with renewable energy generation. In this study, an adaptive principal component analysis (PCA)-enhanced transformer architecture, hereinafter referred to as PCA-Transformer, is developed to enhance the performance of transformer models in predicting renewable energy output. The proposed model uses PCA to dynamically determine and adapt the transformer architecture and prioritize the most informative features from time series data, thereby improving the model\u2019s attention on relevant information and reducing computational burden. This is essential for accurately capturing the intricate temporal patterns and nonlinear relationships that are typical present in renewable energy time series data. The PCA-Transformer enhances the performance of transformer models in sequence-to-sequence predictions by incorporating an adaptive mechanism that customizes their structure based on the best PCA eigenvectors. The architecture adaptively aligns with the underlying patterns in data by adjusting the number of attention heads and critical dimensions within each transformer block. The adaptability of the proposed architecture is crucial for effectively simulating the complex nature of renewable energy generation patterns. The efficiency of the proposed model was evaluated using the Alice Springs Australia DKASC-ASA and EIA Energy datasets. The proposed model has superior forecasting performance than traditional transformer models and cutting-edge renewable energy forecasting methodologies."}, {"paperId": "5d95ee8b9b44effb92c8451b0b6876e7fec478cc", "title": "Advanced Hydroponic Crop Management: A Smart IoT Architecture Leveraging Hybrid and Transformer-Based Deep Learning Models", "year": 2025, "citationCount": 0, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "", "status": null, "license": null, "disclaimer": "Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://api.unpaywall.org/v2/10.1109/SNAMS67467.2025.11390893?email=<INSERT_YOUR_EMAIL> or https://doi.org/10.1109/SNAMS67467.2025.11390893, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. This study presents an advanced IoT-enabled crop management framework that utilizes a hybrid deep learning approach, incorporating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), and Transformer models. By employing sensors to track critical environmental parameters and processing both image data and time-series information, the system facilitates predictive analytics and automated adjustments to optimize growth conditions.
